contract_scope: Client-to-server envelope for /api/auth/login (#A-2). The backend returns an opaque bearer session id that the client MUST attach as `Authorization: Bearer <id>` on subsequent REST and WS calls. Auth is OPTIONAL across the rest of the model-inference surface — callers that omit the header continue to operate anonymously.
honesty_rules: Passwords traverse the wire in plaintext over the /api/auth/login POST body. The demo backend hashes them via password_hash(PASSWORD_DEFAULT) before persistence; plaintext never touches SQLite. Production hardening (TLS termination, rate-limit, brute-force lockout) is explicitly fenced for this demo.
Session ids are opaque 32-char hex strings, NOT JWT. Revocation is a single UPDATE on the sessions table — there is no stateless token to keep consistent.
TTL is clamped server-side to [60s, 30d]. Client-supplied ttl hints are not accepted — the TTL comes from MODEL_INFERENCE_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS (default 12h).
Invalid-credential responses never distinguish wrong-username from wrong-password. The error envelope code is always `invalid_credentials`.
Disabled users (users.status != 'active') cannot log in; the error is `invalid_credentials` (not disclosing account status).

rejection_codes.invalid_credentials: no matching user / wrong password / disabled user

logout.auth: Bearer token required; omitting or expired returns 200 with revocation_state='already_revoked' for idempotency

whoami.auth: Bearer token required; no header or invalid token returns 401 session_expired/session_revoked/invalid_credentials

rejection_codes.session_expired: bearer token's expires_at is in the past
rejection_codes.invalid_credentials: no bearer token or token does not match any session row
